name: Sprint
description: A faster locomotion mode that usually carries a cost, commitment, or noise tradeoff.
design_notes: Sprint changes traversal pacing and encounter spacing. It can be a convenience mode, a stamina-limited escape tool, or a riskier movement state that affects stealth and aim. Its start and stop behavior matter as much as its top pace because players feel sprint through acceleration, camera framing, footsteps, and recovery.
design_purpose: Let players cross known spaces quickly without making baseline movement feel sluggish.
Create pressure through stamina, noise, aim spread, or delayed recovery.
Support chase, escape, and traversal pacing in larger areas.
player_fantasy: The player feels urgent and athletic, choosing when to spend effort for faster repositioning.

parameters.name: sprint_velocity_multiplier
parameters.description: Multiplier applied to baseline locomotion while sprinting.

parameters.name: sprint_acceleration_ms
parameters.description: Ramp window before the sprint modifier reaches full effect.

parameters.name: stamina_drain_per_meter
parameters.description: Resource cost paid by distance covered while sprinting.

parameters.name: sprint_footstep_noise_radius
parameters.description: Noise radius reported to AI perception while sprinting.

edge_cases: Sprint input during reload, crouch, ladder climb, swimming, or aiming needs a clear priority rule.
Stamina exhaustion should transition to normal movement without repeatedly starting and stopping each frame.
Sprint camera effects must blend out before cutscenes, menus, or dialogue take camera control.
AI hearing and player footstep audio should agree on when sprint is actually active.
common_bugs: Sprint drains stamina while blocked by a wall because input is active but movement is zero.
Toggle sprint remains set after stamina exhaustion and restarts unexpectedly when stamina recovers.
Animation stride length does not match locomotion, causing foot sliding.
Sprint modifies aim or FOV on the client but not the authoritative combat state.
balancing_notes: Unlimited sprint is mainly a traversal convenience; stamina sprint becomes a resource decision.
High sprint noise can make stealth routes riskier without forbidding fast movement entirely.
accessibility_notes: Offer hold, toggle, or auto-sprint settings to reduce sustained button pressure.
Avoid using only camera FOV changes to communicate sprint; include posture, audio, or UI state.

implementation_notes.unity: Use an explicit movement-state enum and feed the final desired velocity into CharacterController.Move or Rigidbody movement from FixedUpdate; keep dash and jump assists outside animation callbacks. For Sprint, validate this path with parameters such as sprint_velocity_multiplier and sprint_acceleration_ms.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep the mechanic state on CharacterBody2D or CharacterBody3D, update velocity in _physics_process, and separate input buffering from collision response. For Sprint, validate this path with parameters such as sprint_velocity_multiplier and sprint_acceleration_ms.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use CharacterMovement with a custom movement state or Gameplay Ability; gate activation through prediction-safe input and reconcile movement corrections cleanly in networked play. For Sprint, validate this path with parameters such as sprint_velocity_multiplier and sprint_acceleration_ms.
implementation_notes.web: Represent the mechanic as deterministic state in the simulation loop, then apply resolved displacement before rendering through Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or a custom engine. For Sprint, validate this path with parameters such as sprint_velocity_multiplier and sprint_acceleration_ms.
